One Link
How it works Features Security Live mesh Share Builders About Get One Link
Features

Everything One Link can do, told by One Link.

This page is generated from the live capability advertisement of the One Link daemon at /api/capabilities. We cannot lie on this page. If a feature is here, the daemon ships it. If it is not, it does not.

live from /api/capabilities updated 2s ago
For people

The everyday things.

Messages

Only you and they can read it. Forward secrecy. Edit, delete, react. Read receipts that you control.

Files of any size

Drag a folder. Drag a movie. Drag a hard drive's worth. It goes peer to peer, resumes on reconnect, never copies to a server.

Voice and video calls

End to end encrypted. Direct peer to peer when possible, relayed only if needed. No mystery server in the path.

Shared folders

Drop a file in, it appears on the other side. Versioned, conflict-free, no "the cloud."

Pair a device in five seconds

QR plus five words. The five words mean the math agreed. That is the whole pairing flow.

Your devices act as one

Phone, laptop, tablet, second laptop. Pair once. They share everything, end to end, between every device.

For privacy

Things only One Link does.

Three-hop private routing

One toggle. Sphinx-routed through volunteer relays. Post-quantum blinded at every hop. No one knows who sent what to whom.

Hardware key recognition

Your device recognizes itself when you return, without you signing in, without us storing a single thing about you.

Threshold recovery

Split a secret across friends. Recover it from any threshold. Your account survives a lost phone without surviving a leak.

Field-bound binding

Each session is bound to the physical environment. A stolen key cannot be replayed on a different network without detection.

Duress mode

A second passphrase that opens a believable empty account. The math gives you a way out.

Confidential builds

Every release is built in a sealed environment with attestation. You can verify nobody touched it.

How we compare

Side by side with everyone else.

Honest table. We marked "yes" only where the architecture itself guarantees it. Policy promises do not count.

One Link Signal WhatsApp iMessage Telegram AirDrop Magic Wormhole
No account / email / phoneyesphonephoneapple idphoneyesyes
End to end by defaultyesyesyesyesnoyesyes
No central server in pathyesnonononoyes (local)no
Post-quantum hybrid keysyesnonoPQ3nonono
Optional three-hop routingyesnononononono
Send 50GB+ peer to peeryesnonononolocal onlyyes
Works offline / LAN onlyyesnonononoyesno
Source open and verifiableAGPLGPLnonopartialnoMIT
Reproducible signed buildsyespartialnonononono
Pair devices in 5 secondsQR + SASlinkQRapple idQRtapcode
Free forever, no tieryesyesyesyestiersyesyes
Threshold key recoveryyesnononononono
Confidential-compute attestationyesnonopartialnonono

Last reviewed 2026-05-16. Open a PR if anything in this table is wrong.

Get One Link → Read the threat model
One Link

A free, private network for messages, files, and devices. Owned by no one. Belongs to everyone.

Built in the open. AGPL-3.0. No tracking, no analytics, no cookies. we are one
